div.h2: advance() vs peek() — Cursor Movement Trace on 'x+1'
div.p: Invariant: peek() is a pure read — zero mutations. advance() is the sole mutation point for current, line, column.
advance() is the sole mutation point for current, line, column. Any code that bypasses it (e.g., self.current += 1 directly) silently corrupts position tracking for every subsequent token.
current = 1 ← unchanged line = 1 ← unchanged column = 2 ← unchanged
Fields mutated: current, line, column Returns: the consumed character
Fields mutated: (none) Returns: next char without consuming, or '\0' if current >= len(source)
reads source[1] = '+' current stays 1 column stays 2 returns '+'
is_at_end() → True no read performed current stays 3 returns '\0'
